description:
You are acting as a Dev Ops engineer who has just been asked to prepare a self-contained “mini-operator” workspace that demonstrates good process-management practice when working with Kubernetes manifests.

Your goal is to produce a repeatable, non-interactive solution that can:

1. Lay out a small filesystem hierarchy dedicated to the operator.  
2. Generate two canonical Kubernetes manifest files.  
3. Provide a bash helper script that validates the manifests *in parallel* and writes well-defined log files.  

When you finish, an automated test suite will inspect nothing except the files you create; therefore every file’s *exact* name, location and contents **must** match the specification below.

Directory layout that must exist at the end:

    /home/user/kube-operator/
        ├── manifests/
        │   ├── deployment.yaml
        │   └── service.yaml
        ├── logs/
        │   ├── deployment.yaml.log
        │   ├── service.yaml.log
        │   └── summary.log
        └── run_parallel.sh    ← executable bash script

Detailed file specifications:

1. /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/deployment.yaml  
   Contents (including spacing!):

        apiVersion: apps/v1
        kind: Deployment
        metadata:
          name: nginx-deployment
        spec:
          replicas: 2
          selector:
            matchLabels:
              app: nginx
          template:
            metadata:
              labels:
                app: nginx
            spec:
              containers:
              - name: nginx
                image: nginx:1.21.6
                ports:
                - containerPort: 80

2. /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/service.yaml  
   Contents:

        apiVersion: v1
        kind: Service
        metadata:
          name: nginx-service
        spec:
          selector:
            app: nginx
          ports:
          - protocol: TCP
            port: 80
            targetPort: 80
          type: ClusterIP

3. /home/user/kube-operator/run_parallel.sh  
   • Must start with the shebang line “#!/bin/bash”.  
   • Must be marked executable (chmod + x).  
   • Behaviour requirements when executed from its own directory:
     a) Creates the sub-directory “logs” if it does not already exist.  
     b) Validates both YAML manifests *concurrently* (two parallel background jobs).  
        ▸ For the purpose of this exercise, “validation” is simply the command  
          `echo "VALID: <absolute-path-to-yaml>" > "<log-file>"`  
        ▸ The agent may use “& … wait”, “parallel”, or any other POSIX-compliant mechanism,
          but the operations **must** run in parallel.  
     c) After both jobs finish, it must generate /home/user/kube-operator/logs/summary.log with exactly two lines in this order:

            deployment.yaml:OK
            service.yaml:OK

     d) The script must exit with status 0.

4. /home/user/kube-operator/logs/deployment.yaml.log  
   Must contain exactly:

        VALID: /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/deployment.yaml

5. /home/user/kube-operator/logs/service.yaml.log  
   Must contain exactly:

        VALID: /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/service.yaml

6. /home/user/kube-operator/logs/summary.log  
   Exactly two lines, line-feed terminated, no extra whitespace:

        deployment.yaml:OK
        service.yaml:OK

Checklist — the automated grader will confirm:

• All listed paths exist and are regular files with the specified contents.  
• /home/user/kube-operator/run_parallel.sh is executable (`[ -x … ]`).  
• The log files and summary file exist and contain exactly the specified text.  

Hints (not commandments):  
• The easiest way to prove the jobs ran in parallel is to launch two background subshells with “&” and then “wait”.  
• Remember to use absolute paths in the log lines, **not** relative paths.  
• Do not hard-code “/root” or “~/”; always use “/home/user/…”.

No additional output or commentary is required; the presence and correctness of these files alone will indicate success.
truth:
Expected absolute paths and exact textual contents after successful task completion:

1. File /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/deployment.yaml  
   (see block in public spec – must match byte-for-byte).

2. File /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/service.yaml  
   (see block in public spec – must match byte-for-byte).

3. File /home/user/kube-operator/run_parallel.sh  
   • First line: #!/bin/bash  
   • The script must create two background jobs whose commands each write the string  
     “VALID: <absolute-path>” to the appropriate log file.  
   • The grader will grep the script for “&” or “parallel” to ensure concurrent execution.

4. File /home/user/kube-operator/logs/deployment.yaml.log  
   Content: “VALID: /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/deployment.yaml\n”

5. File /home/user/kube-operator/logs/service.yaml.log  
   Content: “VALID: /home/user/kube-operator/manifests/service.yaml\n”

6. File /home/user/kube-operator/logs/summary.log  
   Content:

        deployment.yaml:OK
        service.yaml:OK

7. The grader will execute:

        /home/user/kube-operator/run_parallel.sh

   and then verify that:
   • Exit code is 0  
   • Both *.log files and summary.log are regenerated with exactly the expected contents.  
   • Execution time difference between the two background validation commands (measured
     by looking at each log file’s modification time) is less than 1 second,
     proving that they ran in parallel.

No other files are consulted during grading.
